import-psc: Before running this script, you should install the PSCompletions module and import it.

invalidName: <@Red>You should enter a valid completion name.
<@Red>You should enter a valid completion name.
success: You can link the completion via <@Magenta>.\scripts\link-completion.ps1 {{ $CompletionName }}<@Green> to get a real-time preview while writing completions.
successWithHooks: You can link the completion via <@Magenta>.\scripts\link-completion.ps1 {{ $CompletionName }}<@Green> to get a real-time preview while writing completions.

invalidName:
<@Red>You should enter a valid completion name.
e.g. <@Magenta>.\scripts\link-completion.ps1 git
<@Cyan>Valid completion names are listed below: 
<@Blue>{{ $completions_list -join ' ' }}
link-completion.exist: <@Red>The completion directory already exists. Please use <@Magenta>psc rm {{ $CompletionName }}<@Red> to remove it first.
linkDone:
<@Green>Completion <@Magenta>{{ $CompletionName }}<@Green> has been linked to the PSCompletions module: <@Magenta>{{ $test_dir }}<@Green>

<@Green>Recommendation: Temporarily disable completion cache via <@Magenta>psc config enable_cache 0<@Green> to preview the latest changes in real-time.
<@Yellow>Note: Completion links are for local use only; <@Magenta>psc add<@Yellow> and <@Magenta>psc update<@Yellow> will ignore them.
